Oja Marketplace

Ifá-Lang Package Ecosystem

Overview

Oja (marketplace) is the official package registry for Ifá-Lang libraries, frameworks, and tools. It provides a centralized location for discovering, publishing, and managing Ifá-Lang packages.

Package Categories

Core Libraries

Essential libraries extending the 16 Odù domains:

  • Extended Math: Advanced mathematical functions
  • String Utils: Enhanced string operations
  • Data Structures: Collections and algorithms
  • Time Utils: Date/time manipulation

Web Development

Libraries for web applications and services:

  • HTTP Servers: Web frameworks and routers
  • Web Clients: HTTP clients and APIs
  • Templates: HTML template engines
  • WebSockets: Real-time communication

Data Science

Tools for data analysis and machine learning:

  • DataFrames: Tabular data manipulation
  • Machine Learning: ML algorithms and models
  • Visualization: Charts and graphs
  • Statistics: Statistical analysis tools

Game Development

Libraries for game creation and graphics:

  • Game Engines: 2D/3D game frameworks
  • Graphics: Rendering and shaders
  • Audio: Sound and music
  • Physics: Collision and dynamics

IoT & Embedded

Libraries for embedded systems and IoT:

  • Sensor Libraries: Hardware sensor interfaces
  • Communication: Wireless protocols
  • Real-time: RTOS integration
  • Power Management: Battery optimization

Development Tools

Tools for development and productivity:

  • CLI Tools: Command-line utilities
  • Testing: Testing frameworks
  • Linting: Code quality tools
  • Build Tools: Build automation

Package Management

Ìjà Package Manager

Ìjà (struggle/conflict) is the official package manager for Ifá-Lang:

Package Management Commands 
# Search packages
ija search "web server"

# Install packages
ija install ifa-web
ija install ifa-database
ija install ifa-auth

# List installed packages
ija list

# Update packages
ija update
ija upgrade ifa-web

# Remove packages
ija remove ifa-web

# Show package info
ija info ifa-web

Project Dependencies

Project Configuration 
# ifa.toml - Project configuration
[package]
name = "my-web-app"
version = "1.0.0"
description = "A web application using Ifá-Lang"
author = "Your Name "

[dependencies]
ifa-web = "1.2.0"
ifa-database = "0.8.0"
ifa-auth = "2.1.0"
ifa-logging = "1.0.0"

[dev-dependencies]
ifa-testing = "0.5.0"
ifa-benchmarking = "0.3.0"

[target.wasm.dependencies]
ifa-web = { version = "1.2.0", features = ["browser"] }

[target.embedded.dependencies]
ifa-web = { version = "1.2.0", features = ["embedded"] }

Popular Packages

Web Development

ifa-web

Full-stack web framework with routing, middleware, and templates.

⭐ 1.2k 📦 50k/week 📝 MIT

ifa-http

HTTP client and server library with async support.

⭐ 890 📦 30k/week 📝 Apache-2.0

ifa-templates

Template engine with inheritance and filters.

⭐ 650 📦 20k/week 📝 MIT

Data Science

ifa-dataframe

DataFrame implementation for tabular data manipulation.

⭐ 2.1k 📦 80k/week 📝 MIT

ifa-ml

Machine learning algorithms and neural networks.

⭐ 1.8k 📦 60k/week 📝 Apache-2.0

ifa-plot

Chart and graph visualization library.

⭐ 950 📦 35k/week 📝 MIT

Game Development

ifa-game

2D game engine with sprites, physics, and audio.

⭐ 1.5k 📦 45k/week 📝 MIT

ifa-graphics

Low-level graphics and shader programming.

⭐ 720 📦 25k/week 📝 Apache-2.0

ifa-audio

Audio synthesis and playback library.

⭐ 480 📦 15k/week 📝 MIT

Publishing Packages

Package Structure

Package Directory 
my-package/
├── ifa.toml          # Package configuration
├── README.md          # Package documentation
├── LICENSE            # License file
├── src/              # Source code
│   ├── lib.ifa       # Main library file
│   └── modules/       # Additional modules
├── examples/          # Usage examples
├── tests/            # Test files
├── docs/             # Documentation
└── scripts/          # Build scripts

Package Configuration

ifa.toml 
[package]
name = "my-awesome-package"
version = "1.0.0"
description = "An awesome Ifá-Lang package"
authors = ["Your Name "]
license = "MIT"
repository = "https://github.com/username/my-awesome-package"
homepage = "https://github.com/username/my-awesome-package"
keywords = ["web", "http", "api"]
categories = ["web-development"]

[dependencies]
ifa-core = "1.3.0"

[dev-dependencies]
ifa-testing = "0.5.0"

[target.wasm.dependencies]
wasm-bindgen = "0.2"

[target.embedded.dependencies]
heapless = "0.8"

Publishing Process

Publish Commands 
# Login to Oja registry
ija login

# Validate package
ija validate

# Dry run (check without publishing)
ija publish --dry-run

# Publish package
ija publish

# Publish specific version
ija publish --version 1.0.0

# Yank (unpublish) version
ija yank 1.0.0

Quality Guidelines

  • Documentation: Complete README and API docs
  • Tests: Comprehensive test coverage
  • License: Clear open-source license
  • Versioning: Semantic versioning
  • Compatibility: Support multiple targets

Ecosystem Integration

IDE Support

  • Babalawo LSP: Auto-completion and error checking
  • Package Discovery: Browse Oja from IDE
  • Dependency Management: Visual dependency graph
  • Documentation: Integrated package docs

Build Integration

  • Cargo Integration: Seamless Rust interop
  • Feature Flags: Conditional compilation
  • Target Support: Multi-platform builds
  • Dependency Resolution: Automatic conflict resolution

CI/CD Integration

  • Automated Testing: Test on multiple targets
  • Automated Publishing: CI/CD pipeline integration
  • Security Scanning: Vulnerability detection
  • Performance Testing: Benchmark integration

Community Guidelines

Package Naming

  • Prefix: Use "ifa-" prefix for Ifá-Lang packages
  • Descriptive: Clear, descriptive names
  • Lowercase: Use lowercase with hyphens
  • Unique: Check for name conflicts

Version Management

  • Semantic Versioning: Follow MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH
  • Breaking Changes: Increment MAJOR version
  • New Features: Increment MINOR version
  • Bug Fixes: Increment PATCH version

Documentation Standards

  • README: Comprehensive project documentation
  • API Docs: Generated from source code
  • Examples: Practical usage examples
  • Changelog: Version history and changes

Code of Conduct

  • Respectful: Treat all community members with respect
  • Inclusive: Welcome contributions from everyone
  • Helpful: Provide constructive feedback
  • Cultural: Respect Yoruba cultural context

Resources

Official Resources

  • Oja Registry: oja.ifa-lang.org
  • Package Search: Search and discover packages
  • Documentation: Package development guides
  • API Reference: Ìjà CLI documentation

Community

  • Forums: Package discussions and support
  • Discord: Real-time community chat
  • GitHub: Source code and issues
  • Blog: Package showcases and tutorials

Tools

  • Ìjà CLI: Package manager
  • Babalawo: Language server with package support
  • Oja Web: Web-based package browser
  • Package Builder: Automated package creation

Deployment Documentation
